reviews.review_msg: Really good prof, very clear, well-organized lectures. it was very obvious that if you paid attention in class and did the readings that you would do well on the exams. Available for extra help in office hours, and gave me very good suggestions for my paper. This wasn't exactly an 'easy' course, but it had very clear expectations on how to do well.

Definitely not a GPA booster, but that's to be expected given the material. I did find her lectures a bit long and slide heavy. However, I think she did a good job with a dry topic, and she was one of the most caring profs I've ever had. Reached out to me when I was struggling at the end of the semester and worked with me to make sure I passed.

Great class, and while there were lots of slides the information related well to the readings. If you did your readings  and attended lectures the slides were easy to understand and weren't overwhelming. Final was open book and fair-- no surprises.
She expects a lot from you, lectures are confusing and misguided, and it is hard to understand what exactly you will be graded on as there is a cumulative 1300 slides plus readings that are tested.
